name: node-brace-expansion
title: node-brace-expansion
summary: Brace expansion as known from sh/bash for Node.js
description:
Brace expansion as known from sh/bash for Node.js
node-brace-expansion provides brace expansion as known from sh/bash for
Node.js. It provides the expand() method to return an array of all possible
and valid expansions of the string argument. If none are found, the string
is returned as the only item in the array.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-brace-expansion because Brace expansion as known from sh/bash for Node.js.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-brace-expansion as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-brace-expansion with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-brace-expansion --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
